assumptions.data: Adjusted daily public US stock histories with ETF risk-factor overlays

coreModels.name: V1 Momentum-Vol Research Model
coreModels.note: 252-day stock momentum; top 3; inverse vol; 10.0% vol target

coreModels.name: V2 HMM-GARCH-MVO
coreModels.note: Two-state Gaussian HMM, GARCH(1,1) likelihood grid and capped long-only MVO

coreModels.name: V3 Boosted Trees + Attribution
coreModels.note: Walk-forward regularized gradient-boosted decision stumps with additive feature attribution

strategies.name: S1 AI Capex Momentum
strategies.note: Self-created AI infrastructure proxy: long quality tech/growth leaders with 12-1 momentum and 3-month confirmation

strategies.name: S2 Quality Growth Barbell
strategies.note: Self-created barbell between compounder quality and growth momentum, with low-volatility ETF as risk factor hedge

strategies.name: S3 Crowding-Aware Momentum
strategies.note: Self-created anti-crowding rule: reward 12-1 momentum but penalize one-month chase and volatility spikes

strategies.name: S4 Low-Vol Alpha Shield
strategies.note: Self-created low-volatility alpha shield: choose stocks with positive medium trend and lower realized risk

strategies.name: S5 Regime-Switch Equity Hedge
strategies.note: Self-created macro regime rule using SPY trend and HYG/IEF liquidity signal to switch between equity and hedge mode

strategies.name: S6 Credit-Liquidity Accelerator
strategies.note: Self-created credit-liquidity accelerator: increase beta only when high yield beats Treasuries with controlled volatility

strategies.name: S7 Revision-Proxy Growth Rank
strategies.note: Self-created earnings-revision proxy using price acceleration, medium trend, and volatility stability when no analyst data is available

strategies.name: S8 Trend-Guarded Reversion
strategies.note: Self-created reversion rule that buys short-term pullbacks only when the long-term stock trend is intact

strategies.name: S9 Sector-Breadth Rotation
strategies.note: Self-created breadth-aware rotation: use ETF factors only to read market breadth, then allocate into stocks

strategies.name: S10 Defensive Growth Stabilizer
strategies.note: Self-created defensive growth stabilizer: quality stocks with automatic hedge sleeve during volatility stress

strategies.name: S11 Tail-Risk Shock Absorber
strategies.note: Self-created shock absorber that reduces stock exposure when drawdown, volatility, or credit stress breaks threshold

strategies.name: S12 Adaptive Volatility Compounder
strategies.note: Self-created adaptive volatility compounder: target risk changes with market stress instead of using one fixed vol target
